asset: glyph set v1 — Invocation crests + V/S/M pips (APPROVED)
note: Approved after 3 review rounds: Eye = heavy-lidded + lash-rays; Bow = heraldic strung, no arrow; Key = horizontal, keystone bow; V = trident-rune + breath-curl; S = paddle hand + punched warding ring; M = cut gem. fill=currentColor, tint via UI tokens. Rejected variants preserved in art/review/glyph-variants/.

Replaces the emoji set (fire/shield/crystal-ball/heart) and the procedural X. Burn = living flame (chosen over scorch-brand variant); Ward = shield + punched keystone (chosen over masonry); Prophecy = hourglass nearly run out. Rejected variants in art/review/glyph-variants/.

Item = strapped satchel (chosen over clasped tome — book icons collide with spellbooks in a wizard game); Gambit = thrown dice pair (chosen over wager coin, which read as a zero at pip size). Rejected variants in art/review/glyph-variants/.

Eye/Bow/Key in ringed medallions inside the regulation ritual circle, gold-leaf #c99a3f on leather-brown #2f2721, still-point at the triangle's center. Small variant: field + one rule + three sigils stacked, reads at 92x128. Bow string thickened in the back's local copies only.

Sealed-spell state marker (Runic Seal & co). Wax blob + engraved rim + punched diamond still-point + one drip; chosen over sealed-scroll (mush at pip size) and binding-chains (reads as infinity at pip size). First imprint draft read as a letter T and was replaced by the diamond. Wired: board SEALED banner (cardSprite.setSealed) + spellbook sealed tag. Rejected variants in art/review/wave2/.

asset: ritual circle round 1 — ALL REJECTED; board-center direction changed
note: ART DIRECTION (2026-07-09): the middle of the board is an ARENA SPACE where the two wizards face off — it will NOT have a ritual circle in it. Supersedes MANIFEST #1's circle half and the bible §11 'separate transparent circle asset' packaging; the regulation-circle motif stays canon in card art/lore (Grandmother Cinder, card back), just not as board furniture. Venue/table art should compose the center as a dueling arena for the two wizard figures.
